strategyMarkdown:
## Kill Target
Druid

## Win Condition
This is a strong matchup for Rogue/Mage. Your Mage 1v1s the Warrior with Frost Novas and slows while you find the Druid. Open with Cheap Shot → Gouge (buying time for Mage to CC the Warrior) → full Kidney Shot. When the Druid trinkets, immediately Blind → Vanish. Resap the Druid, wait for stun DRs to reset, then reopen with Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot for the kill.

---

## Key Notes

- **Never Blind a Druid in Bear Form** — you cannot resap him unless he is in humanoid/caster form. Wait for him to shift out.
- Watch for Warrior **Intervene** on the Druid — if he Intervenes, the next ability that hits the Druid transfers to the Warrior. Your Mage can **wand** the Intervene off without breaking Sap.
- If the Druid sits Kidney Shot without trinketing, pump maximum damage — Hemorrhage spam to stack Wound Poisons. If he uses Nature's Swiftness to heal, Blind him to prevent the recovery.
- Keep resapping the Druid between stun chains to buy your Mage time controlling the Warrior with Frost Nova and Polymorph.
- If you have double Vanish (Preparation), you can handle the Intervene yourself: Cheap Shot transfers to Warrior → Vanish → Cheap Shot Druid directly → Kidney Shot.

## Kill Target
Priest (if not Dwarf)

## Win Condition
Sap the Priest and wait for your Mage to sheep the Warrior. Watch for Warrior Intervene on the Priest — if he Intervenes, wait for it to expire or have Mage wand it off. Open on Priest with Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot. When Priest trinkets, use Evasion and keep applying pressure. Blind the Warrior if he trinkets sheep. Vanish → Garrote (silence) → Kidney Shot for the kill.

---

## Key Notes

- **If the Priest is Dwarf**, consider swapping to kill the Warrior instead — Dwarf Stoneform removes your bleeds (Garrote silence), Wound Poison stacks, and they have higher stun resistance.
- Priest will try to **Psychic Scream** — use Cloak of Shadows or Berserking to avoid it. Trinket fear only when under real pressure.
- If Priest uses **Pain Suppression** early, back off and reset — Blind the Warrior, Vanish, wait for DRs, then reopen.
- Your Mage should Counterspell the Priest's big heals (Greater Heal) during burst windows, not waste it on DoTs.
- Use corners to your advantage to LoS the Warr while pressuring the Priest.

## Kill Target
Either — depends on who you find and the situation

## Win Condition
Openers decide this matchup. The millisecond gates open, **swap to your dagger mainhand** in case you cannot find the enemy Rogue and must engage the Mage. If you find the enemy Rogue, open with Premeditation → Cheap Shot → instant Evasion (25% chance his Blind misses) → 5cp Rupture (not Kidney Shot — Rupture prevents him from trinketing Kidney and resetting). If you cannot find the Rogue, Shadowstep Ambush the enemy Mage repeatedly with Mind-Numbing Poison on your dagger (30% proc, 60% cast time slow).

---

## Key Notes

- **Always use Rupture first, not Kidney Shot** when opening on the enemy Rogue — if you Kidney Shot, he trinkets and resets. Rupture prevents the reset by keeping him in combat with a bleed.
- Coat your dagger with **Mind-Numbing Poison** — the 60% cast time slow on the enemy Mage is huge for protecting your Mage from Sheep and burst.
- When sapping the enemy Mage, **max range the Sap** and Sprint away — this prevents him from trinketing into a Frost Nova on you.
- Let your Mage **Spell Steal** the enemy Mage's Ice Barrier before you open.
- Vanish → Cheap Shot chains are your bread and butter — whoever runs out of Vanishes first is at a huge disadvantage.

## Kill Target
Rogue — or Warrior if Rogue is elusive

## Win Condition
This is a heavy pressure comp that will train your Mage hard. Find the enemy Rogue and open with Cheap Shot → Rupture → Vanish to prevent a reset. Your Mage must Frost Nova and kite the Warrior while Polymorphing the Rogue between your stun chains. Control the Rogue with stun → bleed → Vanish → restun cycles.

---

## Key Notes

- Your Mage is the primary target for their team — Ice Block is a critical survival cooldown, save it for when Rogue + Warrior overlap burst.
- Use **Evasion** if the Rogue opens on you — the 25% dodge chance on Blind is valuable.
- Bleeding the Rogue early prevents Vanish resets — always Rupture before Kidney Shot.
- If the Warrior charges your Mage, Frost Nova → Blink resets distance. Mage must kite aggressively.
- Once the Rogue is controlled with Poly, the Warrior alone cannot survive coordinated burst.

## Kill Target
Priest (if not Dwarf) — or Warlock

## Win Condition
Sprint out and Shadowstep → Sap the Warlock before he gets into combat (Warlocks have good stealth detection and can send their pet to break Sap timing). Open on the Priest with Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot. If Priest uses Pain Suppression, Blind him → Preparation → Vanish → resap. Wait for DRs, reopen with Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot. Use corners to LoS the Warlock while pressuring the Priest.

---

## Key Notes

- **If Dwarf Priest**: do not go for the Priest — Stoneform removes your bleeds and Wound Poison. Switch kill target to the Warlock.
- **Shadowstep Sap the Warlock immediately** — do not give him time to get into combat, as his pet will break your Sap timing.
- **Click off any Mage buffs** if the Warlock has a Felhunter — Devour Magic will consume the buff, reveal your location, and break your stealth, costing you a Vanish.
- Warlock Fear is the primary threat — save Cloak of Shadows to avoid Death Coil, and trinket Fear only when under heavy pressure.
- Your Mage should Counterspell the Priest's big heals, not the Warlock's Shadow casts.
- Use corners to LoS the Warlock's damage while pumping the Priest.

## Kill Target
Druid

## Win Condition
This is a rough matchup for Rogue/Mage. Sprint out and Shadowstep → Sap the Warlock immediately before he gets into combat. Find the Druid and open with Cheap Shot → Gouge (buying time for Mage) → full Kidney Shot. When Druid trinkets, wait for him to leave Bear Form, then Blind → Vanish. Resap the Druid, wait for stun DRs, reopen with Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot. Use Cloak of Shadows to avoid Death Coil.

---

## Key Notes

- **Sap the Warlock first** — Sprint out and Shadowstep Sap before he can get into combat or use his pet to detect you.
- **Click off Mage buffs** if Warlock has a Felhunter — Devour Magic reveals you and breaks stealth.
- Never Blind the Druid in **Bear Form** — wait for him to shift to caster form so you can resap after Blind.
- Cloak of Shadows is critical to avoid Warlock's Death Coil during your burst windows.
- Your Mage just has to survive the Warlock's pressure while you control and kill the Druid.
- Stun DRs must be fully reset before reopening — patience is key in this matchup.

## Kill Target
Warlock

## Win Condition
Sap the Warlock, then find and Sap the enemy Rogue. Instantly Cheap Shot the Warlock → Eviscerate (not Kidney Shot — he will trinket into Death Coil). Get him low as fast as possible before Sap expires on the Rogue. Vanish → Garrote to silence the Warlock. When the Rogue trinkets Sap, Blind him and finish the Warlock.

---

## Key Notes

- **Do not Kidney Shot the Warlock** — he will trinket into Death Coil and reset. Eviscerate for maximum burst instead.
- **Cloak of Shadows** after Kidney Shot to prevent the trinket → Death Coil combo.
- **Pre-Vanish** if you suspect the enemy Rogue is nearby and may have pre-Vanished to find you — if you can't see the Rogue at all near the Warlock, he's likely stealthed aggressively.
- Click off Mage buffs vs Felhunter — Devour Magic reveals you and costs a Vanish.
- Time is of the essence — you must kill the Warlock before the Rogue can peel effectively. Speed > setup in this matchup.
- Your Mage should keep the Rogue controlled with Frost Nova and Polymorph after Sap breaks.

## Kill Target
Either — depends on who you find first

## Win Condition
If you find the Druid first: Cheap Shot → Gouge → Kidney Shot, then when Druid trinkets, wait for caster form → Blind → Vanish → resap. Wait for stun DRs and reopen. If you find the Rogue first: Cheap Shot → Rupture (prevents reset) → Vanish → restun chain. Your Mage controls whichever target you are not on.

---

## Key Notes

- Do not instantly open on the first target you see — **look for both players** before committing. You want to know where both enemies are.
- If Druid comes out while you're on the Rogue: you can swap to the Druid or continue on the Rogue depending on Druid's cooldown state.
- **Never Blind the Druid in Bear Form** — you can't resap him.
- Vanish → Sap the Rogue to control him while finishing the Druid is a strong play if the Rogue opens on your Mage.
- Don't instantly trinket stuns from the enemy Rogue — sit them if you're not in danger. Save trinket for critical moments.
- Your Mage should sheep/control whichever target you're not currently stunlocking.

## Kill Target
Enemy Mage

## Win Condition
Sap the Druid at max range and Sprint away (in case of trinket → Frost Nova). Let your Mage Spell Steal the enemy Mage's Ice Barrier. Open on the enemy Mage with Premeditation → Shadowstep Ambush → Cheap Shot chain. Poly the Druid to prevent healing during burst. Counterspell the enemy Mage during your burst window.

---

## Key Notes

- **Max range Sap** the Druid and Sprint away — if he trinkets into Frost Nova, you don't lose a cooldown.
- Your Mage should **Spell Steal** the enemy Mage's Ice Barrier before the opener.
- Druid HoTs mean you need to burst hard after a Counterspell — don't spread damage, commit to the kill.
- If the enemy Mage Ice Blocks, coordinate to burn it with burst, then finish him when it expires.
- Watch for the Druid shifting out of Polymorph — Druids can shapeshift to break Poly, so time your Polys to cover burst windows rather than spamming them.

## Kill Target
Priest (if not Dwarf) — or Mage

## Win Condition
Sap the enemy Mage. If he trinkets, immediately Cheap Shot the Priest → Blind the Mage. Full Kidney Shot on the Priest. Your Mage Polymorphs the enemy Mage (he has no trinket). Counterspell the Priest's heals during burst. If Priest uses Pain Suppression, Vanish to avoid CC and wait for DRs to reopen.

---

## Key Notes

- **If Dwarf Priest**: consider killing the Mage instead — Stoneform removes your bleeds and Wound Poison.
- Sapping the Mage forces a trinket decision — if he trinkets, you get a free Blind → Sheep chain on him while you kill the Priest.
- If the enemy Mage does not trinket Sap, open on the Priest while the Mage is still sapped.
- Counterspell the Priest's **Greater Heal** during burst, not his instant casts.
- The enemy Mage will try to Counterspell your Mage — position behind pillars to bait the CS.
- Cloak of Shadows avoids Priest's Psychic Scream during your burst window.

## Kill Target
Druid

## Win Condition
You counter this comp. If the Hunter is on his Flare, your Mage can Frost Nova to break the setup. Cheap Shot the Druid → Kidney Shot → Sheep the Hunter (force trinket) → full Blind on Hunter. You now have 8+ seconds of free damage on the Druid. Evasion + Ghostly Strike to dodge the Druid's stuns. Pump him down — there's nothing they can do.

---

## Key Notes

- You **counter this comp** in almost every aspect — it's all about getting a clean setup.
- If Hunter sits on Flare, try to **Sap the Hunter** instead — Druid comes out, you open on him, and Hunter must sit the Sap or trinket into Blind.
- **Evasion + Ghostly Strike** avoids the Druid's stuns in Bear Form.
- Force the Hunter to trinket early (Sheep or Blind) so you can restun him later for free.
- Your Mage should land Sheep on the Hunter during your burst windows on the Druid.

## Kill Target
Hunter

## Win Condition
Sprint out immediately — you must Sap the Priest before the Hunter's pet gets into combat (which negates your Sap timing). Open on the Hunter with Cheap Shot → immediate Evasion (25% chance for ranged abilities to miss). Build Wound Poison stacks with Hemorrhage. Full Kidney Shot — when he trinkets, you're ahead. Blind the Priest to force his trinket. Your Mage lands Sheep on the Priest. Vanish → Cheap Shot the Hunter → second Evasion → Kidney Shot for the kill.

---

## Key Notes

- **Sprint out fast** before the Hunter's pet enters combat — once the pet is in combat, you can't cleanly Sap the Priest.
- Pop **Evasion immediately** after opening — the 25% miss chance on the Hunter's ranged abilities is critical for survival.
- Force the Hunter to **trinket early** (Kidney Shot) — then you can restun him later for free.
- Blind the Priest → force trinket → Mage Sheep chain. Once Priest has no trinket, you control the fight.
- If Priest goes for **Psychic Scream**, Cloak of Shadows to avoid it. Trinket the fear only if under lethal pressure.
- If the Priest is not Dwarf, you can alternatively Sap the Hunter and open on the Priest.

## Kill Target
Hunter

## Win Condition
Find and Sap the enemy Rogue. Open on the Hunter with Cheap Shot → Evasion → Hemorrhage spam to build Wound Poison. Kidney Shot the Hunter to force trinket. Your Mage controls the Rogue with Polymorph after Sap breaks. Blind the Rogue if he comes to peel. Vanish → restun the Hunter for the kill.

---

## Key Notes

- If you find the Rogue first, Sap him and open on the Hunter. If you find the Hunter first, open on Hunter and have your Mage look for the Rogue.
- **Evasion** is strong here — dodge chance works on both the Rogue's melee and Hunter's ranged attacks.
- The enemy Rogue will try to **Blind your Mage** to prevent Poly — your Mage should position carefully behind pillars.
- Avoid the Hunter's **Freezing Trap** — don't walk over known trap locations. If caught, trinket only when Kidney Shot is available.
- Once the Rogue is controlled in Poly, the Hunter dies fast under coordinated burst.

## Kill Target
Hunter

## Win Condition
Sheep the Warrior, open on the Hunter. Cheap Shot → Evasion → Kidney Shot. Frost Nova the Warrior if he trinkets Sheep to prevent him from reaching your Mage. Your Mage applies Frostbolt slow and repolys the Warrior. Vanish → restun the Hunter for the kill.

---

## Key Notes

- Keep the Warrior **Polymorphed** — he's the cleave threat but not the kill target.
- **Frost Nova** the Warrior immediately if he trinkets Poly to prevent him reaching your Mage.
- Hunter's **Scatter Shot** can break your CC chains — stay aware of its cooldown.
- Evasion dodges the Hunter's ranged attacks — use it aggressively during your burst.
- If Warrior Intervenes the Hunter, your Mage wands to break the Intervene before you restun.

## Kill Target
Shaman

## Win Condition
Find and Sap the enemy Rogue. Open on the Shaman with Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot to force trinket. Kick healing spells. Your Mage Polymorphs the Rogue (destroy Grounding Totem first). When Shaman trinkets, continue applying Wound Poison pressure with Hemorrhage. Blind the Rogue if he peels. Vanish → Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot the Shaman for the kill.

---

## Key Notes

- **Grounding Totem** absorbs Polymorph — destroy it before your Mage attempts to Poly the Rogue.
- **Kick every Healing Wave** — Shaman healing is powerful but interruptible. This is your main advantage.
- Shaman's **Earth Shock** interrupts your Mage's Frost school — Mage should avoid standing in Earth Shock range.
- Enemy Rogue's Blind will target your Mage — position Mage carefully behind pillars.
- If the Shaman goes Ghost Wolf to run, Crippling Poison keeps him in range.

## Kill Target
Enemy Mage

## Win Condition
Sap the enemy Mage. Open on the Shaman briefly to force defensive cooldowns, then swap to the enemy Mage. Your Mage Counterspells the enemy Mage during your burst window. Blind the Shaman during the kill attempt. Shaman alone cannot sustain their Mage under coordinated CC + burst.

---

## Key Notes

- **Grounding Totem** eats Polymorph — always destroy it before attempting Poly on the Shaman.
- Shaman's **Wind Shear** interrupts your Mage's Frost school on a short cooldown — your Mage must juke casts.
- **Purge** from the Shaman removes your Mage's Ice Barrier and other buffs — reapply when safe.
- If the enemy Mage Ice Blocks, swap to pumping the Shaman to force cooldowns, then swap back when Block ends.
- Coordinate burst with Counterspell — the enemy Mage dies fast when locked out of Frost school.

## Kill Target
Shaman

## Win Condition
Both targets are viable — setups are extremely important. Try to Sap the Shaman (he may go Ghost Wolf form making Sap impossible). Open with Premeditation → Cheap Shot on Shaman → Evasion (avoid Warrior damage). Mage Frost Novas the Warrior. Full Kidney Shot the Shaman. Kick his heals. If Shaman trinkets, continue pressure. Blind the Warrior → your Mage resheeps. Bleed the Shaman so he can't run. Vanish → Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot for the kill.

---

## Key Notes

- If the Shaman is in **Ghost Wolf** form, you cannot Sap him — you'll be forced to just open directly.
- **Trinket the Warrior's charge** — not a fear — because charge enables a 10-second Dismantle, which is devastating.
- **Kick every heal** — Shaman healing is powerful but fully interruptible. This is how you win.
- Use **Wound Poison + bleeds** to prevent the Shaman from recovering — bleeds hit hard against plate targets too.
- If your Mage gets a clean Sheep on the Warrior while you open on the Shaman, the game is often won right there.
- If Kidney Shot gets **resisted** (Shamans have some resistance), don't panic — apply Expose Armor and keep Hemorrhage pressure.

## Kill Target
Hunter or Shaman — depends on setup

## Win Condition
Sprint out before the Hunter's pet enters combat. Sap the Shaman (or Hunter). Open on the Hunter with Cheap Shot → Evasion → Kidney Shot. Your Mage controls the Shaman with Poly (destroy Grounding Totem first). Kick Shaman heals during burst. Blind the Shaman to force trinket → Mage resheeps.

---

## Key Notes

- **Sprint out fast** before the pet gets into combat — this preserves your Sap timing.
- **Grounding Totem** absorbs Poly — destroy it before CCing the Shaman.
- Shaman's **Earth Shock** locks out your Mage — Mage must juke or stay at range.
- Hunter's **Scatter Shot** → Freezing Trap is their win condition — never stand together, avoid traps.
- If the Shaman is already low and the Hunter is full HP, consider swapping kill target to Shaman.

## Kill Target
Paladin — or Warrior if Paladin is out of reach

## Win Condition
Both targets are viable. Sap the Paladin, have your Mage Sheep the Warrior and Spell Steal Blessing of Sacrifice. Open on the Paladin with Cheap Shot. If he trinkets (a huge mistake), full Kidney Shot → Eviscerate. When he Bubbles, immediately Vanish and Sprint away — wait for Bubble to expire and stun DRs to reset. Come back in with Cheap Shot → Blind the Warrior → Kidney Shot the Paladin for the kill.

---

## Key Notes

- Your Mage should **Spell Steal** Blessing of Sacrifice before you open — it transfers damage to the Warrior otherwise.
- When the Paladin **Bubbles**, do not waste cooldowns — Vanish, reset, and wait for DRs. You cannot damage through Bubble.
- Both targets losing their trinkets is the win condition — track trinket usage carefully.
- If the Paladin is hiding behind corners, you can switch to the Warrior if he's low.
- You cannot outlast Paladin healing on mana — you must create burst windows and commit fully.
- The Warrior's trinket timing matters — if he trinkets Sheep early, your Mage can freely Sheep him again later.

## Kill Target
Rogue

## Win Condition
Sap the Paladin and have your Mage 1v1 the Ret/Rogue since Paladins can't do much against Mages. Wait for a window of opportunity. If you find the Rogue, Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot. Use Cloak of Shadows to avoid Paladin's Hand of Justice stun. If the Paladin BoPs the Rogue, it doesn't matter — your Mage can still damage through BoP with spells. Blind the Paladin during your burst on the Rogue.

---

## Key Notes

- **Cloak of Shadows** resists the Paladin's Hand of Justice stun — use it during your burst window.
- **BoP on the Rogue does not save him** — your Mage's spells go through Blessing of Protection. Only physical damage is blocked.
- If you get sapped by the enemy Rogue, trinket → Blind → Vanish immediately to regain control.
- The Paladin will try to stun you with Hand of Justice — Cloak of Shadows or position out of range.
- Preparation gives you double Vanish — use them aggressively to chain stuns on the Rogue.

## Kill Target
Warlock

## Win Condition
Sap the Paladin, Spell Steal Blessing of Sacrifice. Resap the Paladin if possible. Open on the Warlock with Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot. Vanish → Garrote. Cloak of Shadows to resist Hand of Justice from the Paladin. Blind the Paladin to force trinket. Mage CCs the Paladin while you pump the Warlock. Bleeds hit hard against the Warlock.

---

## Key Notes

- **Spell Steal** Blessing of Sacrifice before opening — otherwise damage transfers to the Paladin.
- **Cloak of Shadows** resists the Paladin's Hand of Justice stun — use it when the Paladin is near you.
- If Paladin **Bubbles** to save the Warlock, wait it out and reset. You can't do anything through Bubble.
- If Paladin **BoPs** the Warlock, your Mage can Spell Steal it or just continue — BoP only blocks physical damage.
- **Click off Mage buffs** vs Felhunter Warlock — Devour Magic reveals you.
- Bleeds (Rupture, Garrote) are strong against the Warlock — keep them rolling.
- Kick the Warlock's Drain Life to prevent sustain.

## Kill Target
Enemy Mage

## Win Condition
Sap the enemy Mage. Open on the Paladin briefly to force Bubble or waste cooldowns, then swap to the enemy Mage once Sap breaks. Your Mage Counterspells the enemy Mage during burst. Blind the Paladin to prevent healing. Force the enemy Mage's Ice Block, then finish him when it expires.

---

## Key Notes

- This is a **LoS-heavy matchup** — force the Paladin to walk out from behind pillars to heal. Paladin can't heal what he can't see.
- Your Mage should **Spell Steal** any Paladin buffs on the enemy Mage.
- If the Paladin Bubbles to heal, you cannot interrupt it — wait for Bubble to end, then re-engage.
- Enemy Mage's **Ice Block** gives one free emergency — plan burst around burning it first, then kill on the second setup.
- Use Cloak of Shadows to avoid the Paladin's Hand of Justice stun during your burst window.

## Kill Target
Hunter

## Win Condition
Sap the Paladin. Sprint out fast before the pet enters combat. Open on the Hunter with Cheap Shot → Evasion → Kidney Shot. Blind the Paladin during burst to prevent healing. Force Paladin trinket → Mage Sheep chain. When both trinkets are down, your setups become free and the Hunter dies.

---

## Key Notes

- **Sprint out fast** to Sap the Paladin before the Hunter's pet gets into combat.
- **Spell Steal** Blessing of Sacrifice from the Hunter before opening.
- Paladin's **Bubble** is the main defensive — when he Bubbles, back off and wait. You can't kill through it.
- Avoid the Hunter's **Scatter Shot → Freezing Trap** combo — don't stand together.
- If Paladin uses **Blessing of Protection** on the Hunter, your Mage can Spell Steal it or continue casting — BoP only blocks physical.
- Once both trinkets are down, coordinate Blind → Sheep on the Paladin while killing the Hunter.

## Kill Target
Rogue (preferred) — or Priest if not Dwarf

## Win Condition
Sap the Priest. If you find the enemy Rogue, Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot. Your Mage Counterspells the Priest. If the Rogue trinkets, you're ahead in cooldowns — Vanish and wait for stun DRs. Reopen with Cheap Shot → Cloak of Shadows (avoid Priest fear) → Kidney Shot. Blind the Priest to force trinket → Mage Sheep → bleed the Rogue so he can't reset → finish him.

---

## Key Notes

- **If the Priest is Dwarf**, do NOT open on him — Stoneform removes bleeds (Garrote silence), Wound Poison, and Dwarves resist stuns more often. Kill the Rogue instead.
- **If the Priest is not Dwarf**, you can go for either target. Opening on the Priest is viable: Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot → Vanish → Cheap Shot → Eviscerate chains.
- Use **Cloak of Shadows** to avoid the Priest's Psychic Scream during your burst.
- Blind the Priest → force trinket → Mage Sheep chain. Once the Priest has no trinket, your setups are free.
- If the Rogue opens on your Mage, don't panic — Frost Nova → Blink resets the situation.
- Always **bleed the Rogue** (Rupture) before going for Kidney Shot — prevents trinket + Vanish reset.
- **Pain Suppression** from the Priest makes the target unkillable temporarily — if used, back off, Blind, and reset.

## Kill Target
Warlock

## Win Condition
Sprint out and Shadowstep → Sap the Warlock before he gets into combat. Open on the Shaman briefly to waste cooldowns, then swap to the Warlock. Cheap Shot → Kidney Shot → Cloak of Shadows (avoid Death Coil). Blind the Shaman during burst. Your Mage interrupts Shaman heals. Kick the Warlock's Drain Life.

---

## Key Notes

- **Shadowstep Sap the Warlock immediately** — do not let him get into combat or use his pet.
- **Grounding Totem** absorbs Poly — destroy it before your Mage attempts to CC the Shaman.
- **Cloak of Shadows** is critical to avoid the Warlock's Death Coil during your burst.
- **Click off Mage buffs** vs Felhunter — Devour Magic reveals you and breaks stealth.
- Shaman's **Wind Shear** locks out your Mage's Frost school — Mage must juke casts.
- **Purge** removes your Mage's buffs — reapply Ice Barrier when safe.
- Warlock Fear is the primary CC threat — trinket only under heavy pressure.
